name: Mrityu Bhaga Degree Activation — Critical Health Danger Day
description: When a transiting malefic planet (Mars, Saturn, Rahu) crosses the Mrityu Bhaga (death degree) of the rising sign or Moon sign, it creates an exact-day trigger for critical health danger.

source_text.quote: The Mrityu Bhaga degrees are: Aries 26°, Taurus 12°, Gemini 13°, Cancer 25°, Leo 24°, Virgo 11°, Libra 26°, Scorpio 14°, Sagittarius 13°, Capricorn 25°, Aquarius 5°, Pisces 12°. When malefics transit these degrees of the Lagna or Moon sign, death-like suffering results.

any_of: Transit Mars crosses the Mrityu Bhaga degree of the natal Lagna sign
Transit Saturn crosses the Mrityu Bhaga degree of the natal Lagna sign
Transit Rahu crosses the Mrityu Bhaga degree of the natal Moon sign
Transit Mars crosses the Mrityu Bhaga degree of the natal Moon sign

interpretation.meaning: Mrityu Bhaga is the most dangerous degree in each sign. When malefic planets transit this exact degree in the Lagna or Moon sign, it creates a pinpoint trigger for critical health events — accidents, sudden illness onset, or surgical emergencies.

metadata.notes: Mrityu Bhaga is degree-specific. This is the most precise timing technique in medical astrology. Each sign has a unique death degree.

name: Vinasha Tara (23rd Nakshatra) — Destruction/Annihilation Trigger
description: When fast-moving planets transit the 23rd Nakshatra from the birth star (Vinasha Tara = destruction star), it triggers the exact timing for health destruction events.

source_text.quote: Vinasha 23rd Star... Triggers destruction, annihilation of health, and sudden medical crises. The most dangerous Tara for physical body.

any_of: Transit Moon passes through the 23rd Nakshatra (Vinasha Tara) from natal Janma Nakshatra
Transit Mars passes through the 23rd Nakshatra (Vinasha Tara) from natal Janma Nakshatra

interpretation.meaning: The 23rd Nakshatra from birth (Vinasha = destruction) is the most dangerous star for health. When fast planets activate it, the body's defenses collapse momentarily — creating exact timing for surgical events, acute medical crises, and health emergencies.

metadata.notes: Vinasha Tara is the single most dangerous Nakshatra transit for health. Used in Muhurta to AVOID medical procedures on these days.

name: Vipat/Pratyak Tara (3rd/5th Nakshatra) — Danger & Obstacle Trigger
description: The 3rd (Vipat = danger) and 5th (Pratyak = obstacle) Nakshatras from birth star create fast triggers for accidents, sudden harm, and obstacles to recovery.

source_text.quote: The 3rd star (Vipat) brings danger and calamity. The 5th star (Pratyak) brings obstacles and hindrances to well-being. Both are inauspicious for health matters.

any_of: Transit Mars passes through the 3rd Nakshatra (Vipat Tara) from natal Janma Nakshatra
Transit Mars passes through the 5th Nakshatra (Pratyak Tara) from natal Janma Nakshatra
Transit Moon passes through the 3rd Nakshatra (Vipat Tara) from natal Janma Nakshatra
Transit Moon passes through the 5th Nakshatra (Pratyak Tara) from natal Janma Nakshatra

interpretation.meaning: Vipat (danger) and Pratyak (obstacle) Taras are classically avoided for all health-related activities. When Mars or Moon transit these, they trigger accidents, sudden injuries, complications in recovery, and obstacles to healing.

metadata.notes: These Taras are universally avoided in Muhurta for surgery scheduling. Their activation during an already-open medical window pinpoints exact crisis days.

name: Mars Anga Gochara — Body-Part Specific Activation
description: Mars transiting specific Nakshatra groups from the birth star activates corresponding body parts for injury, surgery, or disease manifestation (Anga Gochara system).

source_text.quote: Mars transit amongst limbs: 1st-3rd right arm (wounds), 4th-5th head (head injury), 10th-12th chest/heart (cardiac danger), 16th-18th abdomen (surgical abdomen).

all_of: Transit Mars activates a specific body-part Nakshatra group from the natal Janma Nakshatra (Anga Gochara)
any_of: Mars in 1st-3rd from birth star (right arm — injury/surgery to arms)
Mars in 4th-5th from birth star (head — head injury/brain surgery)
Mars in 10th-12th from birth star (chest/heart — cardiac events)
Mars in 16th-18th from birth star (abdomen — abdominal surgery)

interpretation.meaning: The Anga Gochara system maps planetary transits to specific body parts via Nakshatra counting. Mars through these groups pinpoints WHICH body part is vulnerable and WHEN — providing both timing and location specificity for medical events.

metadata.notes: Anga Gochara is unique in providing BOTH timing and body-part localization. Mars is the primary planet used for surgical body-part mapping.

name: Ashtakavarga Pinda Zero-Point Transit — Weakest Vitality Trigger
description: When malefic planets transit through degrees with zero or very low Ashtakavarga points (Pinda) in the Lagna chart, they hit the native's weakest vitality points, triggering health collapse.

source_text.quote: When malefics transit through the signs where the Sarvashtakavarga score is lowest, the native experiences maximum suffering, disease, and danger to life.

all_of: A malefic planet (Mars, Saturn, or Rahu) transits a sign/degree where the Sarvashtakavarga score is 0, 1, or 2 (minimum points)

interpretation.meaning: Ashtakavarga maps the vitality strength of each degree in the chart. Degrees with zero or minimal points represent the native's weakest spots. When malefics hit these exact degrees, the body's defenses collapse at that point, triggering health crises.

metadata.notes: Ashtakavarga provides degree-level precision. Combined with Mrityu Bhaga, these create the most precise fast triggers for medical events.
